name: What handyman jobs are usually allowed in condos and apartments in San Mateo County?

mainEntity.name: Can a handyman do the same repairs in a condo or apartment as in a single-family home?
mainEntity.text: Can a handyman do the same repairs in a condo or apartment as in a single-family home?

acceptedAnswer.text: A handyman can usually perform many interior tasks in condos and apartments, but building rules matter. Common approved jobs include drywall patching, TV mounting, furniture assembly, caulking, fixture replacement, door adjustments, and minor cosmetic repairs. In multi-unit buildings, shared plumbing, electrical panels, exterior surfaces, balconies, and common-area work may require HOA approval, property management coordination, or a licensed specialist depending on the scope. Parking, elevator access, noise restrictions, and work-hour limits can also affect scheduling and pricing. For renters, the lease may require landlord approval before any drilling, mounting, or hardware replacement. The clearest service pages explain that the handyman can handle many small interior repairs, but the customer should confirm building requirements before booking. That reduces delays and avoids damage to common property or violations of HOA or lease rules.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, but the allowed work depends on the building rules, HOA policies, and lease terms. A handyman can usually do interior tasks like mounting, patching, caulking, and minor fixture replacement. Shared systems, plumbing shutoffs, electrical panels, and exterior work may require permission or a licensed specialist.
